title: scanner: witness upsert error silently diverges in-memory tree from DB
description: scanner.rs scan_block() (line ~421): errors from upsert_witness() are caught with if let Err(e) and only logged at warn level. The scan then continues and advances the tip. On the next restart, load_state() restores witnesses from the DB (which has the stale pre-error state), but the commitment tree has already advanced past the point where those witnesses were valid. The restored witnesses are now rooted at a different tree state than the scanner's commitment tree, causing an AnchorMismatch on the next spend attempt. Fix: either treat upsert_witness failures as fatal (return Err and do not advance the tip), or do not advance the tip when any witness upsert fails. The current behavior means a transient SQLite error permanently corrupts the wallet's spendability without any user-visible signal beyond a warn log.
